sanskrit:
अनपेक्षः शुचिर्दक्ष उदासीनो गतव्यथः।
सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः॥
transliteration: anapekṣaḥ śucir dakṣa udāsīno gata-vyathaḥ | sarvārambha-parityāgī yo mad-bhaktaḥ sa me priyaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Not-seeking, pure, skillful, sitting-apart, gone-beyond-suffering, all-enterprises-renouncing, whoever My-devotee, he to-Me dear.
contextual_meaning: Krishna lists the inner posture of the securely-attached devotee: autonomous yet surrendered, competent yet non-striving, clean in motive, emotionally un-triggered, and willing to drop any project that does not serve love.
psychological_state: Secure-autonomous attachment: the nervous system is calm, the self is not fused with outcomes, and spiritual object-constancy is established.
trigger_scenario: You just lost the promotion you worked for. Instead of rumination or revenge, you feel a surprising lightness, update your résumé without self-contempt, and spend the evening volunteering at the shelter because it aligns with your values.
anchor_text: Love without clinging, work without striving, and let every action dissolve into quiet surrender.
